name: Page Header
description: Top-level page header with breadcrumb navigation, page title, optional badge, supporting text, and action buttons.

arrowBack.description: Shows a back arrow for navigating to the previous page.

badge.description: Shows a status badge next to the page title.

supportingText.description: Shows description text below the title row.

actions.description: Shows action buttons on the right side of the header.

do: Use at the top of every page to establish context and navigation
Always include breadcrumbs for pages deeper than the top level
Place primary page-level actions in the actions slot
dont: Don't use more than one page header per page
Don't omit the back arrow on nested pages
Don't place form-level actions in the page header — use a Footer instead

accessibility.keyboard: Tab moves between breadcrumb links and action buttons.
